content.full_text:
FPPC

MAY 1 6 1991
LAW OFFICES OF 11:13 aM

NIELSEN, MERKSAMER,
HODGSON, PARRINELLO & MUELLER
A PARTNERSHIP INCLUDING A PROFESSIONAL CORPORATION

SACRAMENTO SAN FRANCISCO
770 L STREET, SUITE 1200 591 REDWOOD HIGHWAY, #4000 650 CALIFORNIA STREET, SUITE 2650
SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814 MILL VALLEY, CALIFORNIA 94941 SAN FRANCISCO, CALIFORNIA 94108
TELEPHONE (916) 446-6752 TELEPHONE (415) 389-6800 TELEPHONE (415) 989-6800

May 10, 1991


Ms. Jeanne Pritchard
Chief, Technical Assistance
& Analysis Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
Post Office Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807


Re: Request for Confirmation of Telephone Advice

Dear Jeanne:

Last month, I telephoned you regarding the 1991 campaign
filing responsibilities of our client, Daniel P. Levin. Our
inquiry on his behalf was prompted by a phone call Mr. Levin
received from the Redwood City Clerk advising him a Campaign
filing might be due by March 31. The City Clerk asked that he
review his situation with the FPPC.

I advised you that Mr. Levin made independent expenditures
in 1989 and 1990 in support of the qualification of a Redwood
City initiative measure which was titled by the Redwood City City
Attorney as "City owned waterfront land sale proposed initiative
measure (Bayport)". This initiative was titled and the petitions
were circulated between November, 1989 and April, 1990. He
turned in sufficient signatures (over 10,000) to the City Clerk
to qualify the measure for the ballot. The city filed legal
action to keep the measure off the ballot. The court took the
initiative off the ballot, ruling that the initiative, as
drafted, contained constitutional and other deficiencies. No
election on Mr. Levin's initiative was ever held. ¹

¹ Thereafter, in July 1990, Mr. Levin submitted two
initiative measures to the Redwood City City Attorney for
titling. One received a title and summary; once again legal
action was commenced to keep the second measure off the ballot on
grounds similar to the prior legal action. The lawsuit was
successful. Since the initiative that did receive a title and
summary was a companion to the initiative invalidated by the
court, neither was ever placed in circulation or submitted to the
City Clerk. Hence, it is our understanding that no Independent
Expenditure or Major Donor filings were required relative to the
two July initiatives.

Ms. Jeanne Pritchard
May 10, 1991
Page 2

     With our firm's advice and assistance, Mr. Levin filed an
Independent Expenditure and Major Donor Campaign Statement (FPPC
Form 461) covering the period January 1, 1989 through May 2,
1990, ("post submission" deadline); May 3, 1990 through June 30,
1990 (semi annual); and July 1, 1990 through December 31, 1990,
(semi annual).  Since Mr. Levin's last filing, no expenditures
have been made in connection with the measure which qualified for
the ballot but then was removed through court action or two
measures submitted in July, 1990.

     In 1991, the only expenditures made by Mr. Levin have been
to seek legal counsel on possible language for ordinances which,
if adopted, would accomplish the same effect as the initiative.
However, no initiative has been submitted to the city for title
and summary at this time.  There has been no attempt to influence
the voters in connection with any measure.

     In my telephone conversation with you, we agreed that Mr.
Levin's current activity is not an independent expenditure as
that term is defined in Government Code Section 82031 and there
is no "measure" before the voters as discussed in the Fontana
Opinion (2 FPPC Opinions 25, 2/4/76).

     We agreed that based on the above facts, Mr. Levin has no
filing responsibilities as an independent expenditure filer with
the city of Redwood City.  I would appreciate your confirmation
of this advice in writing.

     If you have any questions or I have misstated any of our
discussions, please do not hesitate to contact me.

                                        Kind regards,

                                        Kirk Alan Pessner
                                        Paralegal/Administrator
                                        Political Reports Unit

KAP/cn

cc:  Daniel P. Levin
     Arlyn C. Jorgenson,
          Redwood City Clerk
